arxiv.title: A Survey on In-context Learning
arxiv.abstract: With the increasing capabilities of large language models (LLMs), in-context learning (ICL) has emerged as a new paradigm for natural language processing (NLP), where LLMs make predictions based on contexts augmented with a few examples. arxiv.title: Logic Mill -- A Knowledge Navigation System
arxiv.abstract: Logic Mill is a scalable and openly accessible software system that identifies semantically similar documents within either one domain-specific corpus or multi-domain corpora. It uses advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to generate numerical representations of documents. Currently

arxiv.title: Rethinking with Retrieval: Faithful Large Language Model Inference
arxiv.abstract: Despite the success of large language models (LLMs) in various natural language processing (NLP) tasks, the stored knowledge in these models may inevitably be incomplete, out-of-date, or incorrect. arxiv.title: Application Of ADNN For Background Subtraction In Smart Surveillance System
arxiv.abstract: Object movement identification is one of the most researched problems in the field of computer vision. In this task, we try to classify a pixel as foreground or background. arxiv.title: Unpacking the "Black Box" of AI in Education
arxiv.abstract: Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have sparked renewed interest in its potential to improve education. arxiv.title: Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network

arxiv.title: DensePose From WiFi
arxiv.abstract: Advances in computer vision and machine learning techniques have led to significant development in 2D and 3D human pose estimation from RGB cameras, LiDAR, and radars. arxiv.title: Hospital transfer risk prediction for COVID-19 patients from a medicalized hotel based on Diffusion GraphSAGE
arxiv.abstract: The global COVID-19 pandemic has caused more than six million deaths worldwide. Medicalized hotels were established in Taiwan as quarantine facilities for COVID-19 patients with no or mild symptoms. arxiv.title: Sample-Efficient Unsupervised Domain Adaptation of Speech Recognition Systems A case study for Modern Greek
arxiv.abstract: Modern speech recognition systems exhibits rapid performance degradation under domain shift. This issue is especially prevalent in data-scarce settings, such as low-resource languages, where diversity of training data is limited. arxiv.title: Pseudo-Inverted Bottleneck Convolution for DARTS Search Space
arxiv.abstract: Differentiable Architecture Search (DARTS) has attracted considerable attention as a gradient-based neural architecture search method.
